message:
I've been doing a little spring refresh on our home safety stuff, and I realized I didn't actually *know* what the best practices are for fire safety—like, beyond "don't leave the stove on." Turns out there's some pretty straightforward stuff that makes a real difference, and it's way less overwhelming than I thought. Ready.gov has a solid breakdown of the basics that actually applies to most homes.

What's one fire safety thing you feel pretty confident about in your place?
